meta.description: A precision design system for personal finance interfaces — optimized for data clarity, trust signaling, and rapid number scanning.

light.name: --mono-color-brand
light.usage: Primary brand/action color for light theme CTAs, active states, and emphasis.

light.name: --mono-color-brand-hover
light.usage: Hover/pressed state of primary actions in light theme.

light.name: --mono-color-positive
light.usage: Semantic positive value color for income and favorable deltas.

light.name: --mono-color-positive-subtle
light.usage: Subtle positive background for chips and low-emphasis status indicators.

light.name: --mono-color-negative
light.usage: Semantic negative color for overspend and destructive patterns.

light.name: --mono-color-negative-subtle
light.usage: Subtle negative background for warning rows and badges.

light.name: --mono-color-warning
light.usage: Warning state color used near budget thresholds.

light.name: --mono-color-bg
light.usage: Primary page canvas in light theme.

light.name: --mono-color-surface
light.usage: Default card/panel background in light mode.

light.name: --mono-color-elevated
light.usage: Elevated/secondary container background in light mode.

light.name: --mono-color-surface-secondary
light.usage: Sub-surface for inset areas, row hovers, and muted blocks.

light.name: --mono-color-text-primary
light.usage: Primary text for headings, key values, and important labels in light mode.

light.name: --mono-color-text-secondary
light.usage: Secondary text for descriptions, metadata, and less prominent labels in light mode.

light.name: --mono-color-text-muted
light.usage: Muted tertiary text for footnotes, placeholders, and helper hints.

light.name: --mono-color-text-inverse
light.usage: Inverse text on filled brand/negative action backgrounds.

light.name: --mono-color-border
light.usage: Default border and divider color in light mode.

light.name: --mono-color-border-strong
light.usage: High-emphasis border for focus rings and selected states in light mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-brand
dark.usage: Primary brand/action color for dark theme CTAs, active states, and emphasis.

dark.name: --mono-color-brand-hover
dark.usage: Hover/pressed state of primary actions in dark theme.

dark.name: --mono-color-positive
dark.usage: Semantic positive value color for income and favorable deltas.

dark.name: --mono-color-positive-subtle
dark.usage: Subtle positive background for chips and low-emphasis status indicators.

dark.name: --mono-color-negative
dark.usage: Semantic negative color for overspend and destructive patterns.

dark.name: --mono-color-negative-subtle
dark.usage: Subtle negative background for warning rows and badges.

dark.name: --mono-color-warning
dark.usage: Warning state color used near budget thresholds.

dark.name: --mono-color-bg
dark.usage: Primary page canvas in dark theme.

dark.name: --mono-color-surface
dark.usage: Default card/panel background in dark mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-elevated
dark.usage: Elevated/hovered container background in dark mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-surface-secondary
dark.usage: Sub-surface for inset areas and muted blocks.

dark.name: --mono-color-text-primary
dark.usage: Primary text for headings, key values, and important labels in dark mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-text-secondary
dark.usage: Secondary text for descriptions, metadata, and less prominent labels in dark mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-text-muted
dark.usage: Muted tertiary text for footnotes, placeholders, and helper hints.

dark.name: --mono-color-text-inverse
dark.usage: Inverse text on high-contrast light chips in dark mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-border
dark.usage: Default border and divider color in dark mode.

dark.name: --mono-color-border-strong
dark.usage: High-emphasis border for focus rings and selected states in dark mode.

typography.name: --mono-text-heading
typography.usage: Section headings such as account names, dashboard panel titles, and modal headers.

typography.name: --mono-text-subheading
typography.usage: Sub-section labels, card titles, and group headers within transaction lists.

typography.name: --mono-text-body
typography.usage: General body copy for descriptions, insight narrative text, and form helper text.

typography.name: --mono-text-label
typography.usage: Uppercase or sentence-case labels on badges, category chips, input field labels, and table column headers.

typography.name: --mono-text-caption
typography.usage: Timestamps, footnotes, and secondary metadata that should recede visually.

typography.name: --mono-text-mono-figure
typography.usage: Monospaced (font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace) style for all currency amounts, percentages, and numeric data to ensure column alignment and digit clarity.

typography.name: --mono-text-mono-figure-lg
typography.usage: Large monospaced variant for primary balance displays on account summary headers where the number is the focal point.

typography.name: --mono-text-mono-figure-sm
typography.usage: Small monospaced variant for inline figures inside table rows, sparkline axis labels, and compact gauge readouts.

components.description: Displays the headline financial state of a single account — its name, institution, current balance, and a 30-day delta. Emit this component when the user asks about their balance, net worth, a specific account, or opens the home dashboard.

props.name: accountName
props.description: Human-readable account label (e.g. 'Chase Checking', 'Vanguard Roth IRA') displayed as the card heading.

props.name: institutionLogo
props.description: URL to the bank or brokerage logo shown as a 32×32 rounded thumbnail left of the account name.

props.name: balance
props.description: Current account balance in the user's base currency, rendered using --mono-text-mono-figure-lg.

props.name: currency
props.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. 'USD') used to format the balance with the correct symbol and locale.

props.name: delta30d
props.description: Signed numeric change in balance over the past 30 days; positive values render in --mono-color-positive, negative in --mono-color-negative.

props.name: accountType
props.description: Account category ('checking' | 'savings' | 'investment' | 'credit' | 'loan') used to select the icon and contextual footer copy.

props.name: lastSynced
props.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last data sync, formatted as a relative string ('2 min ago') in --mono-text-caption / --mono-color-text-muted.

components.trigger_intent: What's my checking account balance? / Show me my Chase balance / How much do I have across all accounts?
components.visual_notes: Card is 100% width with 24px horizontal padding and 20px vertical padding on a white (#FFFFFF) surface with a 1px --mono-color-border border and --mono-radius-lg corners. Top row: 32×32 rounded institution logo on the left, accountName in --mono-text-heading / --mono-color-text-primary, and accountType badge (pill, --mono-radius-full, --mono-color-surface-secondary background) on the right. Middle: balance in --mono-text-mono-figure-lg centered or left-aligned with currency symbol in --mono-color-text-secondary. Below the balance: delta30d shown as '↑ $312.40 (2.4%)' or '↓ $88.00 (0.6%)' in --mono-text-mono-figure-sm colored by sign. Bottom: lastSynced in --mono-text-caption / --mono-color-text-muted. No interactive states on the card itself; tapping navigates to account detail.

components.description: Renders a scrollable, date-grouped list of debit and credit transactions for one or more accounts. Emit when the user asks to see recent spending, searches for a specific merchant, or requests their transaction history.

props.name: transactions
props.description: Array of transaction objects each containing { id, date, merchantName, category, amount, currency, direction: 'debit'|'credit', pending: boolean }.

props.name: groupByDate
props.description: When true (default), transactions are segmented under sticky date headers ('Today', 'Yesterday', 'April 22') sorted newest-first.

props.name: showCategory
props.description: When true, a category chip (e.g. 'Food & Drink') appears below the merchant name in --mono-text-label / --mono-color-text-muted.

props.name: highlightMerchant
props.description: If provided, rows whose merchantName matches this string receive a --mono-color-accent left border to surface a search result.

props.name: maxRows
props.description: Caps the visible list length and appends a 'Show all N transactions' link; omit to render all rows.

props.name: currency
props.description: Base currency code used to format all amount values uniformly across the list.

components.trigger_intent: Show me my recent transactions / What did I spend at Whole Foods last week? / List all my expenses this month
components.visual_notes: Outer container is a white card with --mono-radius-lg and --mono-color-border border. Date group headers are sticky (position: sticky, top: 0) in --mono-text-label uppercase / --mono-color-text-muted on a --mono-color-surface-secondary background with 8px vertical padding. Each transaction row is 64px tall with 16px horizontal padding, a 44×44 merchant icon (rounded square, --mono-radius-sm, gray placeholder if no logo), merchant name in --mono-text-body / --mono-color-text-primary, and a right-aligned amount in --mono-text-mono-figure colored by direction: credits use --mono-color-positive, debits use --mono-color-text-primary (not red — red is reserved for errors). Pending rows render the amount in --mono-color-text-muted with a 'Pending' label. A 1px --mono-color-border divider sits between each row. Rows are tap-targets that expand to a detail sheet.

components.description: A compact inline area or line chart visualizing a time-series value (balance, spending, net worth) over a selected period. Emit when the user asks how a value has changed over time, requests a trend, or opens an account detail view.

props.name: data
props.description: Array of { date: string (ISO 8601), value: number } data points plotted left-to-right along the x-axis.

props.name: period
props.description: Time range label ('7D' | '1M' | '3M' | '6M' | '1Y' | 'ALL') displayed in a segmented control above the chart and used to label the x-axis.

props.name: trend
props.description: 'up' | 'down' | 'flat' — controls the line and gradient fill color: up → --mono-color-positive, down → --mono-color-negative, flat → --mono-color-accent.

props.name: height
props.description: Pixel height of the chart canvas; defaults to 80 for inline sparkline contexts, use 160 for expanded detail views.

props.name: showAxes
props.description: When true, renders x-axis date labels and y-axis value gridlines; false renders a clean borderless sparkline with no labels.

props.name: highlightLast
props.description: When true, places a filled circle at the final data point with a floating label showing the current value.

components.trigger_intent: How has my savings balance changed this year? / Show me my spending trend over the last 3 months / Chart my net worth
components.visual_notes: The chart sits inside a white card with no border in inline contexts. The SVG path is a smooth cubic bezier curve (not straight segments) with a gradient area fill beneath it: the gradient starts at 20% opacity of the trend color at the curve and fades to 0% at the bottom. The line stroke is 2px. The period segmented control sits flush to the top-right of the card in --mono-text-label; the active segment has an --mono-color-accent underline, not a filled pill. If showAxes is true, gridlines are 1px dashed --mono-color-border at 25/50/75% y positions, and date labels appear at start/mid/end of the x-axis in --mono-text-caption / --mono-color-text-muted. The highlight dot is 8px diameter, filled with the trend color, with a white 2px stroke so it pops from the line. Tooltip on hover/tap shows date + formatted value using --mono-text-mono-figure-sm on a --mono-color-brand background with --mono-color-text-inverse text.

components.description: Shows spending progress toward a budget limit for a category or overall monthly budget, using a horizontal bar gauge with contextual color signaling. Emit when the user asks how much budget they have left, whether they are on track, or views a budget breakdown.

props.name: categoryName
props.description: Budget category label rendered as the gauge title (e.g. 'Groceries', 'Dining Out', 'Total Monthly').

props.name: spent
props.description: Amount spent so far in the current period; used to calculate fill percentage against the limit.

props.name: limit
props.description: The budget ceiling for the period; the gauge fill equals (spent / limit) clamped to 100%.

props.name: currency
props.description: ISO 4217 currency code for formatting the spent and remaining values displayed below the bar.

props.name: period
props.description: 'monthly' | 'weekly' | 'annual' — appended to the remaining label as context ('$240 left this month').

props.name: showProjection
props.description: When true, a secondary dashed marker on the gauge bar shows projected month-end spend based on daily burn rate.

props.name: categoryIcon
props.description: Emoji or icon name displayed left of categoryName to improve scanability in a stacked list of gauges.

components.trigger_intent: Am I on track with my budget this month? / How much do I have left for groceries? / Show me my budget breakdown
components.visual_notes: Each gauge is a horizontal row inside a white card. Layout: left side has the categoryIcon (20px) + categoryName in --mono-text-subheading, right side has the spent/limit fraction in --mono-text-mono-figure / --mono-color-text-secondary. Below the label row is the gauge track: a full-width rounded bar (height 8px, --mono-radius-full) in --mono-color-surface-secondary as the empty track. The fill bar overlays it with --mono-radius-full on both ends and color driven by fill percentage — 0–74%: --mono-color-positive, 75–99%: --mono-color-warning, 100%+: --mono-color-negative with a subtle pulse animation. If showProjection is true, a 2px dashed vertical tick mark in --mono-color-text-muted appears at the projected percentage. Below the bar: '$ remaining this [period]' in --mono-text-caption / --mono-color-text-muted left-aligned and the percentage right-aligned in the same style. When overbudget, the remaining label switches to 'Over by $X' in --mono-color-negative. Multiple gauges stack with --mono-space-4 gaps.

components.description: Surfaces a single AI-generated financial observation — a spending anomaly, savings opportunity, or behavioral pattern — in a scannable card format with optional action. Emit when the assistant has detected a meaningful financial pattern or the user asks for recommendations, tips, or a financial summary.

props.name: insightType
props.description: 'anomaly' | 'opportunity' | 'pattern' | 'milestone' — controls the leading icon and accent strip color on the card's left edge.

props.name: headline
props.description: One short sentence (max ~60 chars) summarizing the insight — the first thing the user reads, styled in --mono-text-subheading.

props.name: body
props.description: 2–3 sentence explanation of the insight with specific numbers — rendered in --mono-text-body / --mono-color-text-secondary.

props.name: metric
props.description: Key figure to highlight in a callout chip (e.g. '+$134 vs last month') using --mono-text-mono-figure styled by sign.

props.name: actionLabel
props.description: CTA text for an optional ghost button at the card bottom (e.g. 'Set a budget', 'See transactions').

props.name: actionHref
props.description: Internal route or callback identifier the actionLabel button navigates to when tapped.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: When true, an × icon appears in the top-right corner allowing the user to hide this insight permanently.

components.trigger_intent: Do you notice anything unusual about my spending? / Give me a financial tip / What should I know about my money this week?
components.visual_notes: Card is white with --mono-radius-lg and a 1px --mono-color-border border. A 4px solid left-edge accent strip (inset, not a separate element — use border-left) signals insightType: anomaly → --mono-color-negative, opportunity → --mono-color-positive, pattern → --mono-color-accent, milestone → --mono-color-warning. Top row: a 20×20 icon matching insightType (left) and an optional × dismiss button (right, 16px, --mono-color-text-muted). Below: headline in --mono-text-subheading / --mono-color-text-primary. Then body in --mono-text-body / --mono-color-text-secondary with --mono-space-2 gap. If metric is provided, it sits in a pill chip (--mono-radius-full, --mono-color-surface-secondary background, 8px horizontal padding, 4px vertical padding) in --mono-text-mono-figure colored by sign — positive green, negative red, neutral accent. If actionLabel is provided, a ghost button (no fill, 1px --mono-color-accent border, --mono-color-accent text, --mono-radius-sm) sits flush-left at the bottom with --mono-space-4 top margin.

components.description: A single-purpose call-to-action button for initiating financial operations such as transfers, payments, budget creation, or navigation. Emit inline whenever the assistant is recommending an action the user can take immediately.

props.name: label
props.description: Button text rendered in --mono-text-label weight 600; should be a concise verb phrase (e.g. 'Transfer Funds', 'Set Budget', 'View All').

props.name: variant
props.description: 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'ghost' | 'destructive' — controls fill, border, and text color treatment.

props.name: size
props.description: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' — sm is 32px tall for inline use, md is 44px for standard actions, lg is 56px for prominent full-width CTAs.

props.name: icon
props.description: Optional icon name (from the Mono icon set) rendered at 16px left of the label with 8px gap.

props.name: loading
props.description: When true, replaces the label and icon with a 16px spinner and disables pointer events to prevent double-submission.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, renders the button at 40% opacity with cursor: not-allowed and suppresses all interaction events.

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: When true, the button stretches to 100% of its container width; default is fit-content.

components.trigger_intent: How do I set up a budget? / I want to move money to savings / Help me pay this bill
components.visual_notes: Variant styles: 'primary' — background --mono-color-accent, text --mono-color-text-inverse, no border, hover darkens background by 8% using filter:brightness(0.92); 'secondary' — background --mono-color-surface, 1px border --mono-color-border-strong, text --mono-color-text-primary, hover background --mono-color-surface-secondary; 'ghost' — no background, no border, text --mono-color-accent, hover shows --mono-color-surface-secondary background; 'destructive' — background --mono-color-negative, text --mono-color-text-inverse, use sparingly for irreversible actions. All variants use --mono-radius-sm for sm/md sizes and --mono-radius-md for lg. Horizontal padding is 16px (sm), 20px (md), 24px (lg). The label is always --mono-text-label weight 600 — never all-caps. Focus ring is a 2px --mono-color-accent outline with 2px offset. Loading spinner is a 16px SVG circle with a 2px stroke and a 270° arc, animated at 0.8s linear infinite rotation.
